title: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo

originalTitle: Bolivia launches early-morning crackdown on roadblocks outside La Paz
title: Bolivia launches early-morning crackdown on roadblocks outside La Paz
content: The government of Rodrigo Paz has sent about 3,500 soldiers and police to clear protesters demanding his resignation.

originalTitle: German police assault protesters at Nakba anniversary rally
title: People Sharing Feelings in a Faraway City
content:
Hello, my amazing young friends from all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, ready to take you on another adventure! Today, we are going to learn about something that happened very far away, in a big city called Berlin. Berlin is in a country called Germany. It's a land across the ocean, in Europe!

A Special Day to Remember

Every year, people sometimes gather to remember very important things. They might remember a happy birthday, or a special holiday. But sometimes, people gather to remember sad things that happened a long, long time ago. They do this so that everyone can learn from the past. And so that sad things do not happen again. In Berlin, thousands of people gathered. They were remembering a very, very old story. This story happened 78 years ago. That is a very long time! It was even before your grandparents were born. Many, many families had to leave their homes back then. They left their houses, their gardens, and their villages. This made them feel very, very sad. It was a big change in their lives. People still remember this sad day today. They call it "Nakba." It means a big sadness or a big change. They gather to remember these families. They want everyone to know that these sad things happened. And they hope that no one ever has to leave their home in such a sad way again. It is like remembering a special part of history. Did you know? Germany is famous for its delicious bread and many wonderful fairy tales, like the stories of Hansel and Gretel!

People Sharing Their Hearts

When people gather like this, it is usually to share their feelings. They might want to tell everyone that they are still sad about something. Or they might want to ask for something to change. This is called a "protest." It means people are speaking up together. In Berlin, the thousands of people were holding signs. They were talking to each other. They wanted to make sure their voices were heard. They wanted everyone to understand their old, sad story. And they wanted people to be kind to each other today. It is very important for people to be able to share their hearts. Everyone should have a chance to say what they believe is right. People were sharing their message in the big city of Berlin. They came from many different places to do this. Did you know? Berlin is a very big city with lots of museums and parks, just like some big cities in Africa, like Cairo in Egypt or Johannesburg in South Africa!

When Police Come to Help

When many people gather in one place, the police are usually there too. Police officers are special helpers in our communities. They wear uniforms and help keep everyone safe. They make sure rules are followed. Sometimes, the police need to make sure people are safe. They might need to guide traffic. Or they might need to ask people to move from one place to another. This is part of their job. They want to make sure everything stays peaceful and orderly. But this time, things got a little tricky. The news said that the police told some of the people that they could not stay in that spot. They wanted the people to move away from where they were gathered. Some of the people did not want to move. They felt it was important to stay there. They wanted to keep sharing their message. They believed they had the right to stand there and speak. Did you know? Police officers train very hard to help their communities and keep people safe from harm!

What Happened in the Streets

Because some people did not want to move, the police tried to make them go. The news told us that the police used some things to help them. This part sounds a little sad. The news said they used something called "pepper-spray." Imagine a mist, like from a water bottle, but it's very, very spicy! If it gets in your eyes, it makes them sting and water. It's not a nice feeling at all. The police used this to make people feel uncomfortable so they would move. The police also touched some people. They guided them to move away from the area. This meant some people were pushed or pulled gently to leave. It was not a very happy time for some of the people who were there. Everyone hopes that when people gather, everything can stay calm and peaceful. It is always best when people can talk to each other kindly. But sometimes, things can get difficult. Did you know? In many countries around the world, people have a special right called "freedom of speech." This means they can share their ideas and feelings peacefully!

Why This Story Matters to Us

You might be wondering why this story from far-away Germany is important to us in Africa. Well, my friends, it teaches us some big lessons! It teaches us that people all over the world have feelings. They care about their history. They want to be heard when they are sad or when they want things to be fair. It shows us that remembering the past is important. We can learn from history so we can make a better future for everyone. It also reminds us that we should always try to talk kindly to each other. Even when we disagree, we can try to understand why someone feels the way they do. When people gather, they want to share their hearts. And we should always listen with kindness. Even if we are far away in our beautiful continent of Africa, we can understand that people everywhere want to live in peace. We can learn to be kind to everyone we meet, no matter where they come from. We can learn to listen to stories, even sad ones, with empathy. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries, all with their own special cultures and stories, just like the many countries in Europe! So, my wonderful friends, we learned about people in Germany remembering a sad day. We learned about how people share their feelings. And we learned that being kind and listening is always the best way. Keep learning, keep growing, and keep being kind! Until next time, Jambo!
summary: People in Germany gathered to remember a sad day from a long time ago, and when they shared their feelings, the police tried to make them move, reminding us that everyone wants to be heard and we should always be kind.
whyMatters: This story helps us learn that people all over the world have important feelings they want to share, and it's always good to listen with kindness and try to understand each other.

originalTitle: Two presidential campaign staffers killed in Colombia as elections near
title: Two presidential campaign staffers killed in Colombia as elections near

content: Xabi Alonso may now be heading to Chelsea, but Mohamed Salah's outburst keeps the pressure on Arne Slot, writes Phil McNulty.

originalTitle: No trophy in turbulent season but Alonso arrival offers Chelsea hope
title: No trophy in turbulent season but Alonso arrival offers Chelsea hope
content: From the gloom of an FA Cup final loss amid a turbulent campaign, Xabi Alonso's impending arrival gives Chelsea hope, writes Nizaar Kinsella.

originalTitle: From non-league to FA Cup hero - Semenyo caps rise with 'incredible' goal
title: From non-league to FA Cup hero - Semenyo caps rise with 'incredible' goal
content: The FA Cup tends to produce fairytale stories and Antoine Semenyo's sensational winning goal in this season's final provided the latest in chapter in the storied history of the world's oldest cup competition.
